rows.description: Recepte anni 1758 Nov. 2do ad Annum 1759 ad vndem
rows.notes: Top title line; handwriting partly cramped and the final word ("vndem"/"adundem") is abbreviated/unclear. Vertical ruling not relevant for this header. Chose this transcription as closest match to the visible letters 'Recepte anni 1758 Nov. 2do ad Annum 1759 ad vndem'.
Clear short line immediately under the title. Letters 'Diem resolutum Nov. 2d' are legible; no amounts present.

Amount column alignment used the underlined subtotal as reference. Pounds look like '656' though the first digit has some overwriting/smudging; shillings '1' and pence '5' are reasonably formed but pence tail slightly faint. Chose 656/1/5 as best reading; if the first digit were different it would still be a 6xx value but the middle two digits are clear.
Description reads clearly as 'Debitum anni procedentis'. Amounts align well in the pound/shilling/pence columns: pounds '239', shillings '8', pence '9' are sharp and pass currency validation.
Subtotal line (no left-hand description visible, only the summed amounts under the two lines above). Numbers '925 / 9 / 10' are well-aligned under the three ruling columns and clear.
Label 'Deductis solutis' is legible. Amounts '857 / 16 / 5' are clear and align to the pounds/shillings/pence columns; all currency values are in valid ranges.

Final remainder line reads 'Restant solvendo a' with amounts '67 / 13 / 5'. There is an annotation to the right 'Dno computante' (see note) — amounts themselves are clear though the tiny abbreviation to the right is somewhat faint; currency validation ok.

This row duplicates the visible line where the right-hand annotation 'Dno computante' is written beside the remainder. The annotation is faint and abbreviated; included here as part of the same physical line because it appears on the same horizontal level. Lower confidence because the abbreviation reading ('Dno computante') is uncertain.
Centered statement approving the computation. Handwriting clear and no amounts present.
First signature line: 'Franciscum Upton Decanum' reads clearly as the dean's name and title. No monetary amounts.

Second signature line is partly unclear: first name looks like 'Jacobum' but the surname is faded/ambiguous ('Co[s]perat' is my best reading of the letter shapes). The title 'Burs. seniorum' (bursar of the seniors) is plausible from the abbreviation but the surname reading is uncertain.
Third signature line reads 'Carolum Webber Bur: Junr' (Bursar Junior). Surname 'Webber' is fairly clear; the 'Bur: Junr' abbreviation is legible though slightly cramped.

Fourth signature appears as 'Hend: Mountsteven Moderat'r' (abbreviated for 'Henry/Henderson'? and 'Moderator'). The surname and office are reasonably readable though the abbreviation forms reduce certainty slightly.
Signature 'Benji Kennicott A.M.' is clearly written and legible. No amounts present.
Final signature line at bottom is short and somewhat ambiguous. Looks like 'Engr. May' or possibly 'Emg. May' — chosen 'Engr. May' as best fit for the letter shapes. No monetary values.
